text.title: AWS S3 Bucket Security
text.summary: Block public access rules, encryption options (SSE-S3/KMS/DSSE-KMS, April 2026 SSE-C change), and bucket policy HTTPS enforcement.

rule.statement: Block all public access at both account level (S3 Block Public Access) and individual bucket level; set Object Ownership to BucketOwnerEnforced (disables ACLs); never grant public read via bucket policy.
rule.rationale: S3 misconfiguration (public access) is among the top causes of cloud data breaches. Account-level blocking provides a backstop even if per-bucket settings are misconfigured. ACLs are a legacy mechanism superseded by bucket policies.

rule.statement: Enable SSE-KMS with a customer-managed KMS key for buckets containing sensitive, regulated, or production data; use SSE-S3 (AES-256) for general non-sensitive buckets; enable S3 Bucket Keys to reduce KMS API call cost by up to 99%.
rule.rationale: SSE-KMS provides audit trails of key usage (CloudTrail), key rotation, and access policy control. SSE-S3 is free and requires no key management. Bucket Keys batch multiple object operations into a single KMS call.

rule.statement: Starting April 2026: SSE-C (customer-provided keys) is disabled by default for new buckets. If SSE-C is required, explicitly enable it via PutBucketEncryption API before any SSE-C upload.
rule.rationale: AWS changed the default to prevent misconfigured SSE-C usage where key management falls entirely on the customer with no AWS-side recovery. Teams migrating workloads must re-enable it explicitly.

rule.statement: Add a bucket policy denying all s3:* actions where aws:SecureTransport is false; this enforces HTTPS for all access including IAM principals with s3:GetObject permission.
rule.rationale: HTTP requests to S3 transmit data and credentials in cleartext. The SecureTransport condition denies HTTP even if IAM policies grant access, enforcing encryption in transit at the bucket level.

rule.statement: Use VPC Gateway endpoints for S3 access from EC2, ECS, Lambda, and EKS; configure endpoint policies to restrict access to specific bucket ARNs.
rule.rationale: Gateway endpoints are free and route S3 traffic over the AWS backbone rather than the public internet. Endpoint policies prevent the endpoint from being used to access unauthorized buckets.

antipattern.description: S3 buckets with public read access enabled via bucket policy or ACL.
antipattern.reason: Public buckets are indexed by search engines and security scanners within minutes of creation. Even accidentally exposed sensitive files become permanently cached and accessible.
